refactor: merge daemon into Tauri as single runtime

- Daemon crate gains a [lib] target; init_app_state() extracted for reuse
- AppState gains log_event_callback so Tauri emits log:{task_id} events
- GUI src-tauri embeds AppState directly via tauri::Manager::manage()
- HTTP handlers replaced by #[tauri::command] functions (tasks, settings, logs)
- WebSocket log stream replaced by Tauri listen() events on the frontend
- fetch()/WebSocket in client.ts/websocket.ts replaced with invoke()/listen()
- CreateTask/EditTask/DeleteTask use Arc<dyn TaskRepository> (removes lifetimes)
- LABALABA_DATA_DIR env var controls data file location; make dev passes repo root
- Standalone daemon binary unchanged and still works

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs/policy: add defensive front-run IO governance rails, policies, and CI gates (#18293)

### Motivation
- Establish a defensive-first governance baseline for the proposed
front-run IO capability bands to reduce dual-use, privacy, and
attribution risks.
- Make segment-level constraints and absolute prohibitions explicit so
implementations cannot introduce individual profiling or adversarial
optimization by accident.
- Provide discoverability and auditability via prompt/task metadata and
a roadmap entry so work is tracked and evidence-gated.

### Description
- Added `docs/io/front_run_bands.md` describing the three capability
bands (phase-structured campaigns + narrative market, cognitive-layer
defense, proof/wargame) and defensive guardrails, evidence requirements,
and MAESTRO alignment.
- Introduced `policy/dual_use_policy.md` (absolute prohibitions and
enforcement hooks) and `policy/segment_governance.yaml` (allowed segment
features and prohibited inferences) to codify constraints.
- Implemented lightweight CI gate scripts
`ci/policy_checks/dual_use_policy_gate.py` and
`ci/policy_checks/segment_policy_gate.py` that fail when required policy
statements/fields are missing.
- Registered the prompt and task
(`prompts/io/front-run-governance-rails@v1.md`, `prompts/registry.yaml`,
`agents/examples/FRONT_RUN_GOVERNANCE_RAILS_TASK.json`) and updated
`docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to track the initiative.

### Testing
- No automated tests were executed as part of this change; the PR
supplies CI gate scripts that CI should run to validate presence and
content of the new policy artifacts.
- The intended verification is for CI to run the new
`ci/policy_checks/*` scripts (and existing doc/schema checks); those
runs were not performed locally in this PR.

### Motivation

- Introduce the Governed Multi-Agent OS as a first-class, evidence-first
governance substrate to capture the policy graph, provenance graph,
per-agent RBAC/approvals, auditor-facing verification (Auditor Verify),
SLOs/dashboards, and security model.
- Align the repo roadmap and readiness artifacts to the Summit Readiness
Assertion and make operational governance guarantees visible to
reviewers and stakeholders.

### Description

- Add a new documentation set under `docs/governed_os/` with the
following files: `overview.md`, `policy_graph.md`,
`provenance_graph.md`, `auditor_verify.md`, `slo_and_dashboards.md`, and
`security_model.md` describing architecture, invariants, and enforcement
points.
- Update `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to insert a
`governed-multi-agent-os` initiative entry (status: `in_progress`,
owner: `codex`) with a readiness-aligned revision note.
- Commit created the docs and roadmap update as a docs-only, low-risk
change.

### Testing

- Automated tests: none executed for this PR because the change is
documentation-only; CI will run standard repo checks on merge.
- Local verification: confirmed files were created under
`docs/governed_os/` and `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` contains the new
initiative, and the changes were successfully committed.

commit.message:
Fix duplicate account bug: single-user guard, data merge, telegram linking

Root cause: student logged in with 2 Google accounts, creating 2 user docs.
Both had telegram_chat_id=null, causing _get_student_uid to return random
user via non-deterministic limit(1) fallback. Weekly reports showed
inconsistent data (XP 190 vs 80).

Fixes:
- Add require_registered_user dependency to all endpoints (P0 guard)
- Handle TOCTOU race on first login (check existing_doc.id == uid)
- Block second Google account with 403 wrong_account
- Auto-link telegram_chat_id from telegram_links on first profile create
- Fix _get_student_uid fallback: order_by(created_at) for determinism
- Frontend: handle 403, show Ukrainian warning, sign-out resilience
- Data merge script: REST API, merges exercise_progress/sessions/achievements
- Diagnostic script for Firestore data inspection

Data migration executed:
- Merged Ch2MR...nCh1 (xp=80) into m9su3...feF2 (xp=190→220)
- Set telegram_chat_id=630005842 on canonical user
- Deleted duplicate user doc and orphaned achievements

Codex review: 4 cycles, APPROVE_WITH_CONDITIONS (P2 deferred: mock
semantics, tg link ordering). 174 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire scheduler loop into API server via quorum adapters

The scheduler loop was fully implemented but never spawned in the
server binary. Add QuorumStateReader and QuorumCommandSink adapters
that read cluster state and propose placement decisions through Raft,
then run the scheduler loop concurrently with gRPC/REST services.

commit.message:
Add auto-update prices from Yahoo Finance via Vercel serverless proxy

- Create api/quotes.js serverless function using yahoo-finance2 to fetch live prices
- Add refreshPrices() to AppContext that updates TICKER_DB and holdings on load
- Add "Refresh Prices" button and dynamic date display in price notice bar
- Export TICKER_SYMBOLS from tickerDb.js for batch fetching
- Static prices remain as fallback if API call fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore Promise.all in AppContext and fix conditional useId() hooks

- AppContext: replace broken results.map()/safeData() shim (from previous
  tool session) with the correct Promise.all([...supabase queries]).
  This was causing 17 TypeScript build errors and a complete data-fetch
  failure at runtime.
- Input, Select: call React.useId() unconditionally then use it as a
  fallback, fixing a React Rules-of-Hooks violation where the hook was
  called inside a short-circuit expression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: workflow scheduler with run history, stop, costs, logs & eval

- Add scheduled_jobs and scheduled_job_runs tables (migrations 023-026)
- Scheduler service with gocron: cron-based triggers, manual trigger, stop
- Run history tracking per execution with iteration folder detection
- Stop running jobs: cancels agent/workflow contexts, updates status
- Stale run cleanup on server restart for both jobs and run entries
- Schedule UI: create/edit/delete schedules with group selection (required)
- Runs panel: expandable run history with inline cost/token display
- Live cost refresh for running jobs, detected iteration folders
- Evaluation popup (ClipboardCheck icon) per run alongside logs
- Schedule count badge on workflow toolbar CalendarDays icon
- Pause/resume UX: clear states with tooltips on all actions
- Fix Supabase UpdateScheduledJob $N parameter offset bug
- Remove workspace_browser from custom tools UI

commit.message:
docs: add Switchboard wedge documentation set (#18290)

### Motivation

- Codify the Switchboard consumer → white‑label → enterprise wedge so
engineering, security, and product teams share a single, evidence-first
design intent.
- Surface key non‑negotiables (receipts-by-default, deny-by-default
policy, signed skills, scoped tokens, tenant isolation) and align the
wedge with MAESTRO threat-modeling expectations.
- Provide a concise reference for launching PR1–PR7 workstreams
(receipts, policy engine, skills supply chain, trust dashboard,
connectors, tenancy).

### Description

- Added consumer/roadmap and feature docs at
`docs/switchboard/POSITIONING.md`, `docs/switchboard/POLICY_MODES.md`,
`docs/switchboard/RECEIPTS_AND_REPLAY.md`,
`docs/switchboard/SKILL_TRUST_MODEL.md`, and
`docs/switchboard/WHITE_LABEL_ADMIN.md` that define positioning, policy
modes, receipt/evidence formats, skill trust ladder, and admin overlays.
- Added a threat model at `docs/security/THREAT_MODEL_SWITCHBOARD.md`
that maps threats to MAESTRO layers and prescribes mitigations (signed
bundles, sandbox probes, deny-by-default policy, scoped tokens,
deterministic receipts, redaction defaults).
- Updated `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to add the `switchboard-wedge`
initiative and bump the initiatives `total` to reflect the new entry.
- This change is documentation-only and does not modify runtime code or
configuration artifacts.

### Testing

- No automated tests were run because this is a documentation-only
change.
- Standard repo CI will run its normal checks on merge and should be
used to validate linting/formatting if required.
- Risk is marked as low and no runtime regressions are expected from
these additions.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_6988132de4dc832e864595c8e329d0a5)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: initial release — 4-tier Linux performance benchmark pipeline

7-dimension rubric (0-28) scoring algorithmic scaling, CPU cycles,
L1/LL cache, branch prediction, memory profile, and ASM metrics.
Uses Valgrind, perf stat, pytest-benchmark, and GNU time.

Includes SKILL.md for Claude Code integration, 6 reference docs,
and a single-file Python pipeline script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reconcile dashboards with actual PaciNet/PacMate metrics

metric names from the actual codebase exports:

heartbeat latency, policy hash, leader status). Replaced with
real metrics like pacinet_nodes_by_state, pacinet_counter_evals_total,
pacmate_active_incidents, etc.

commit.message:
fix: raise z-index of tooltips

Floating overlay elements (tooltip, dropdown, popover, hover card, context menu)
had z-index values (50–1000) at or below the modal backdrop (1000), causing them
to be clipped when rendered inside a modal. Raised all affected components to
z-index 1010 (backdrops to 1009) so they always render above modals and drawers.

New z-index hierarchy:
  Toast         9999 (unchanged)
  Tooltips      1010 (was 1000)
  Dropdowns     1010 (was 100)
  Popovers      1010 / 1009 backdrop (was 50 / 49)
  HoverCards    1010 (was 50)
  ContextMenus  1010 / 1009 backdrop (was 100 / 99)
  Drawer        1001 (unchanged)
  Modal         1000 (unchanged)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CWAizuerdr9mCUg6QT6fXh

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Enable cross-origin resource loading for mobile devices

Add crossOrigin="anonymous" attribute to all media elements (img, audio, video)
to enable proper CORS handling for resources loaded from R2 Worker CDN.

- Add crossOrigin to topic page thumbnails
- Add crossOrigin to AudioPlayer and VideoPlayer components
- Fix slug matching priority in dictation practice (exact match > transcript count)
- Remove invalid <head> tag in layout to fix hydration error
- Remove reference to non-existent default audio file
- Add debug console page for mobile testing

This fixes:
- Cover images not loading on mobile
- Audio/video not playing on mobile
- Wrong material being loaded when slug matches multiple candidates
- Hydration errors during page load

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui): add Realtime Events tab to team detail with structured event cards

Add a new "Realtime Events" tab in team detail page that displays WS events
in real-time via a centralized Zustand store + wildcard listener. Includes
structured card components for delegation, task, message, agent, and CRUD
events with agent name resolution from TanStack Query cache. Each card is
clickable to show raw JSON with syntax highlighting and copy support.

Also removes byte-based task truncation in delegation event payloads that
was corrupting multi-byte UTF-8 characters — now sends full task content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: fix 524 timeouts — statement_timeout, getStores CTE, dashboard chat_stats via review_chat_links

- Add statement_timeout: 30s to DB pool to prevent infinite query hangs
- Rewrite getStores() from N+1 subqueries (260 scans) to CTE + LEFT JOIN
- Optimize getDashboardStats chat_stats: use review_chat_links (~700 rows) instead of chats→products OR join (300K+ rows)

docs: governed personal agent integration reference architecture (#18288)

### Motivation
- Provide an engineer-ready, governance-aligned reference architecture
for the Personal Agent integration pattern (Switchboard → Maestro →
IntelGraph / CompanyOS) so implementers have concrete API shapes, event
contracts, audit/evidence expectations, and a two-week thin-slice plan.
- Record this spec in the project roadmap so cross-team work
(connectors, policies, audit) is discoverable and tracked.

### Description
- Added `docs/integrations/REFERENCE_ARCH_v0.1.md` describing
invariants, the canonical message envelope, Personal Agent API endpoints
(`POST /agent/run`, `POST /agent/run-playbook`, `GET /runs/{run_id}`),
Switchboard webhook contracts, Maestro orchestration contract,
IntelGraph/CompanyOS client contracts, audit writer/evidence bundle
shapes, a minimal repo layout, thin-slice implementation plan, MAESTRO
security alignment, and a forward-leaning `policy-aware run-spec
compiler` enhancement.
- Updated `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to reference the new integration
spec and bumped `last_updated`/`revision_note` to reflect the change.
- Files modified/added:
  - `docs/integrations/REFERENCE_ARCH_v0.1.md` (new)
  - `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` (updated)

### Testing
- No automated tests were run because this is a documentation-only
change (no runtime code modified).

Rewrite weeks 7-8 with organic pedagogical flow and rich notebooks

Week 7 (Model Evaluation) — complete rewrite, 46 slides:
- Organic flow: sklearn basics → train/test variance → model complexity
  (polynomial degree, decision tree depth) → bias-variance → hyperparams →
  train/validate/test → K-fold CV → variants (stratified, time series,
  grouped, LOO) → data leakage → learning/validation curves
- 4 new generated figures (polynomial complexity, decision tree depth,
  train/validate/test split, bias-variance curve)
- New 45-cell in-class demo notebook with matplotlib visualizations

Week 8 (Tuning, AutoML & Tracking) — restructured, 38 slides:
- Tuning (grid/random/Bayesian) → nested CV → AutoML (AutoGluon) →
  PyTorch reproducibility → experiment tracking (W&B/MLflow)
- Rewritten 32-cell notebook with grid/random/Optuna comparison,

* fix: use keyboard navigation in SelectField test for JSDOM compatibility

Base UI's Select popup doesn't fully open in JSDOM (hidden attribute
persists on the positioner), so getByRole("option") can't find accessible
options. Switch to keyboard navigation (ArrowDown + Enter) which works
reliably in the test environment.

  setPersonProperties in app-sidebar and use-ab-palette

- Rename siteSubNav to SITE_SUB_NAV (SCREAMING_CASE convention)
- Add request body type guard in schema-sync HTTP endpoint and
  sanitize internal error messages
- Refactor permanentlyDeleteUser into batched scheduled mutations
  to avoid unbounded mutation size
- Wrap useAnalytics return in useMemo for referential stability
- Move @no-mess/client to dependencies in no-mess-cli package

commit.message:
fix: Allow Project Admins to create projects on the Projects page

The "New Project" button and empty state help text were gated behind
isSuperAdmin(), making them invisible to Project Admins. Now uses
role-aware canCreateProject (Super Admin + Project Admin) for the
create button and canDeleteProject (Super Admin only) for delete.

docs: add Summit vs OpenClaw one-pager and update roadmap status (#18287)

### Motivation
- Publish a concise competitive positioning asset that articulates
Summit's governed differentiation versus OpenClaw and record its
completion in the project roadmap.

### Description

one-page positioning sheet and updated `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json`
(updated `last_updated`, `revision_note`, added
`competitive-openclaw-onepager` initiative, and adjusted summary
counts).

### Testing
- No automated tests were required for this documentation-only change;
CI/docs checks are expected to run in the normal pipeline if configured.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_6988135e679c832eb7d23ddfca176280)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(delegation): serialize announces per session to prevent stale history reads

Concurrent announce runs (delegate + subagent) on the same leader session
could read stale session history because the agent loop reads at start and
writes at end. Added per-session mutex via sync.Map to serialize announces,
ensuring each reads up-to-date history.

Also removed unnecessary task preview truncation in delegation events
(WS clients handle display formatting).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: bump version to 0.27.0, update CHANGELOG and DESIGN.md

- pyproject.toml: version 0.26.0 → 0.27.0
- CHANGELOG.md: add v0.27.0 entry documenting all task cancellation changes
- DESIGN.md: add §10.22 research record (cites Kubernetes Pod deletion grace
  period, Go context.Context, Java Future.cancel(), POSIX SIGTERM/SIGKILL)
- DESIGN.md §11: mark "Task cancellation" as complete (v0.27.0, 497 tests)

Add migration 0019 to drop and rebuild markets, predictions, bets, bet_audit_log

Migration 0018 recorded as applied but tables remained broken on Turso.
This migration drops and recreates all four affected tables from scratch
with the full current schema, ensuring a clean slate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Homepage: add countdown to next session above session card

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: 29 tests for task cancellation (v0.27.0)

New tests in tests/test_task_cancellation.py (29 tests):
- Agent.interrupt() default no-op returns False
- cancel_task() returns False for unknown task_id
- cancel_task() queued: returns True, removes from queue
- cancel_task() queued: publishes task_cancelled with was_running=False
- cancel_task() queued: does not affect other queued tasks
- cancel_task() in-progress: returns True
- cancel_task() in-progress: adds to _cancelled_task_ids
- cancel_task() in-progress: calls agent.interrupt()
- cancel_task() in-progress: publishes task_cancelled with was_running=True
- _route_loop: cancelled RESULT discarded, not in _completed_tasks
- _route_loop: no workflow callback for cancelled RESULT
- WorkflowManager.cancel(): sets status="cancelled"
- WorkflowManager.cancel(): sets completed_at
- WorkflowManager.cancel(): unknown id returns empty list
- WorkflowManager.on_task_complete(): no-op after cancel
- WorkflowManager.on_task_failed(): no-op after cancel
- cancel_workflow(): cancels all queued tasks
- cancel_workflow(): unknown id returns None
- cancel_workflow(): partially-done tasks in already_done
- REST DELETE /tasks/{id}: queued 200, in-progress 200, unknown 404, unauth 401
- REST DELETE /workflows/{id}: known 200, unknown 404, unauth 401
- ClaudeCodeAgent.interrupt(): sends C-c to pane, returns True
- ClaudeCodeAgent.interrupt(): no pane returns False
- In-progress cancel with retries: cleans up _active_tasks on RESULT discard

Updated tests/test_task_cancel.py: test_cancel_dispatched_task_returns_false
→ test_cancel_dispatched_task_returns_true (semantics changed: in-progress
tasks are now cancellable, returning True)

Total: 497 tests (was 468).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add governed agent integration reference architecture (#18285)

### Motivation
- Provide an engineer-handoff reference that captures canonical APIs,
event topics, connector contracts, OPA policy gating, hash-chained
audit/receipt requirements, and delivery slices for a governed
SIEM→Agent→SOAR integration.
- Record the artifact in the project roadmap and register the prompt
that produced the document to ensure provenance and evidence-first
governance.

### Description
- Add `docs/architecture/governed-agent-integration-reference.md`
containing front-door APIs (`/agent/run`, `/agent/run-playbook`,
`/runs/{run_id}`, `/agent/approve`), event envelope and topic names,
Splunk/ServiceNow/Slack connector contracts, OPA preflight examples,
audit writer/receipt minimum fields, swap matrix, and recommended
delivery slices.
- Update `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to add the
`governed-agent-integration-reference` initiative, update `last_updated`
and `revision_note`, and increment the roadmap summary counters
(`rc_ready` and `total`).
- Add prompt artifact
`prompts/architecture/governed-agent-integration-reference@v1.md` and
register it in `prompts/registry.yaml` with the computed `sha256` to
capture the prompt-scoped provenance.
- This change is documentation-only and does not modify runtime
services, code paths, or deployment manifests.

### Testing
- No automated tests were run because this is a documentation-only
change.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_698817c5adb8832eb504257f56e62015)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update npm.spec.ts to reflect manifestName removal

Commit f194c08 removed manifestName from NpmPackageManager since the
PackageManager interface made it optional. The test was still asserting
it equals "package.json" — updated to expect undefined.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: velnora
repository.description: Velnora is a framework-agnostic application platform built around architectural clarity, composability, and long-term scale. It enables seamless multi-app orchestration, type-safe routing, and predictable bootstrapping without locking developers into a single framework. Minimal magic. Explicit design. Ecosystem-first thinking.

commit.message:
feat: add DELETE /tasks/{id} + DELETE /workflows/{id} endpoints (v0.27.0)

- DELETE /tasks/{task_id}: cancels queued or in-progress tasks via
  orchestrator.cancel_task(); returns 200 {cancelled, task_id, was_running}
  or 404 if unknown/already-completed
- DELETE /workflows/{workflow_id}: cancels all tasks in a workflow via
  orchestrator.cancel_workflow(); returns 200 {workflow_id, cancelled,
  already_done} or 404 if unknown
- Updated OpenAPI schema snapshot to include new DELETE endpoints

Design: REST DELETE on resource URI follows Kubernetes kubectl delete pattern;
both endpoints require auth (session cookie or X-API-Key). DESIGN.md §10.22.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
